description: Enrichment analysis pipeline via gget: run gget enrichr over a gene list across 5 databases (pathway/ontology/transcription/diseases_drugs/celltypes), write per-database CSVs + a summary, then attach ARCHS4 tissue-expression for the first genes. Writes CSVs under record_store (read-only remote queries).
rules: read-only — only queries Enrichr + ARCHS4 and writes local files
needs network and an installed gget package (plus pandas); with gget absent the porter degrades gracefully and still writes a non-empty report
all outputs land under record_store (the porter runs the vendored core there)
plotting is disabled by default (the porter passes --no-plot) to stay headless
usage: Set GENE_LIST (path to a file with one gene symbol per line, or a CSV with genes in the first column) and SPECIES (default human; also mouse/fly/yeast/worm/fish). Output: enrichment.log (run log) plus enrichment_<db>.csv per database, enrichment_summary.csv, and enrichment_expression.csv.
limitation: Requires gget + pandas + network for real enrichment results; offline it only produces the run log. Per-database failures are warned and skipped. Output prefix is fixed to 'enrichment'.
